items.question: What does the Style Consistency AI workflow do?
items.answer: This workflow helps you apply a consistent visual style across many assets. It is designed as a style consistency generator so color, line, texture, and overall feel stay unified across your images.

items.question: What kind of style reference images should I upload?
items.answer: Use one or more images that clearly represent the style you want to match—such as existing brand illustrations, campaign visuals, or product photos with the right treatment. These references act as the visual language guide for new outputs.

items.question: Can I restyle new content to match an existing brand look?
items.answer: Yes. You can upload style references and then describe the new scenes, products, or diagrams that should match that style. The workflow focuses on harmonizing the look while respecting the underlying subject matter.

items.question: How is style consistency different from subject consistency?
items.answer: Style consistency focuses on the overall visual language—line work, color, texture, and rendering—while subject consistency focuses on keeping a specific character or product on-model. You can use both workflows in combination, but this template is dedicated to unifying style across assets.

items.question: What are common use cases for AI style consistency?
items.answer: Teams use this workflow to keep illustrations, icons, marketing images, and documentation diagrams visually aligned, even when different people or tools are creating the inputs.

items.question: How do credits and costs work for Style Consistency generations?
items.answer: Each generation consumes credits depending on the model and configuration. A good approach is to first test a small set of assets to confirm the style match, then scale up once you are confident in your reference setup.
